name: builders-stack

scripts.dev: echo 'Run ./tilt_up.sh (not tilt up) — Tilt owns dev servers, not Nx. See docs/nx.md for the Tilt-vs-Nx split.'
scripts.check: nx run-many -t typecheck lint test && bun run check:seo

$comment: Pin ONE @types/react across the tree (Expo SDK 53 / RN 0.79 baseline). @types/react 19.2.x tightened ReactPortal to require `children`, which react-native 0.79's bundled JSX types reject → 'View/Text cannot be used as a JSX component' in apps/mobile. 19.0.x is the version RN 0.79 was built against and is valid React-19 types for web/ui too. A floating ^19 hoists 19.2.x and reintroduces the break, so pin the minor.
$comment-security-overrides: Force patched versions of transitive deps flagged by osv-scanner — all reached only through build-time/dev tooling, never in a deployed Worker or client bundle. Verified: `bun run build` passes and `osv-scanner scan` reports 0. axios (via nx; 10 advisories incl. GHSA-gcfj-64vw-6mp9 CVSS 8.3 — nx@23.x still pins vulnerable 1.16.x so an override is the only fix), body-parser (via express), brace-expansion / dompurify / fast-uri / postcss / sharp / svgo (assorted transitives; forced to their fixed releases, API-compatible with the lines they replace). The `next: ^16.2.11` devDependency is not a root app dep — it hoists a patched next so the ^16.2.x copies pulled by @nx/next and @better-auth/cli (dev tooling) resolve to the fixed version; the deployed apps keep their exact 16.3.0-preview.9 pin. Runtime deps get fixed, not ignored — see osv-scanner.toml for the dev-only advisories that can't be cheaply bumped.
